80 Brhat Samhitn
is disastrous for the world. This may be the reason why Mercury

If towns are besieged when Mercury gets eclipsed, they
would be freed from the siege when Mercury rises again. Accor-
ding to other scholars besieged cities would be benefited only
when Mercury rises in the west.
[Cf.
5^: ii
According to this authority the besieging army becomes victorious
or takes possession of the town when Mercury rises in the west,
whereas when he rises again in the east the town would be retaken
or freed from the enemy's hold. The metre is
